instructions.content: State the decision as a precise yes/no or A-vs-B question before doing anything else.
Before spawning any reviewers, investigate the actual repo/codebase to gather concrete, checkable grounding facts. Put these facts verbatim in the brief every reviewer receives.

Assign genuinely opposing roles — Advocate (for) and Skeptic (against) at minimum, plus Migration/Risk when the change has real implementation surface — never a 'balanced/neutral' framing for all reviewers.
Spawn all reviewers in parallel — single message, N parallel tool calls — never sequentially.

After collecting all reviews, synthesize a verdict that names which argument held up and why, rather than presenting all opinions unweighted. Render an actual recommendation: proceed, do_not_proceed_for_now, or proceed_with_modification.
If the verdict is do_not_proceed_for_now, name a concrete revisit trigger — a specific condition, not a vague 'later'.
Offer to persist the verdict as a .context/findings/*.md entry using the context-file skill.
NEVER assign all reviewers a neutral/balanced framing — this produces false consensus instead of real tension.
NEVER let reviewers debate from assumption instead of investigated facts — ground the brief first.
NEVER present three opinions without a synthesized verdict that picks a side.
